item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 200 replies — 332.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 200 • Updated: 2026-07-04 00:38 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170

>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

item.title: >if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT — 79 replies — 316.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-07-04 01:02 UTC

OP

>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Early replies (first 3)

62433628
But saaaaaar isnt this normal in india and all civilized places? Only in the so called united states of america they kick their kids out

62433637
>>62433628
anything that isnt jewish is indian because.... because it just is, okay!!!!

62433641
All you need is a half million dollar down payment and you can get your mortgage payment down to $4,250 per month on your loan against a million dollar house. Then you can live in the desert on top of your neighbors with trees that will fall over during the first rains and slight breeze because they don't belong in a desert.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441547
>>62433637
I was referring to his grammar.

62441559
>>62433626
Boomers are entitled to this piece of land. There is nothing you can do about it.
You own nothing you happy.

62442042
>>62441268
These retards (a LARPer, obviously, but there are people who genuinely believe this shit) don't realize that their kids are competing with someone who's parents are professionals from Nigeria, Syria, or some other 3rd World shithole hell-bent on their kids having a better life than they had, who will do everything to ensure they start out on third base, while you're keeping your kid in the on-deck circle.

62442046
>>62442042
>professionals from Nigeria, Syria, or some other 3rd World shithole
And you can bet they're not going to spend their retirement (and their kids' inheritance) going on cruises or pursuing other boomer money-sinks, probably viewing themselves as a failure if they don't leave their kids the biggest inheritance they can.

62442134
>>62442046
Yes, Nigerians are well known globally for their fiscal responsibility and sense of duty.

62442194
in my early 20s my dad told me that he was not going to support me from here on out, and that i needed to become totally independent. he made it abundantly clear, i was totally on my own.

so i said okay, and i spent my 20s basically grinding in college with basically zero support from him. i took on student loans, pursued a stem degree, got internships, i spent dozens of hours a week studying- with no social life. after all, why would i party, date, or make friends in college when those would, objectively speaking, only distract from my coursework? which would, objectively speaking, run the risk of imperiling my career prospects and financial future? in other words, why would i take on frivolous social activities when i had no fallback plan in case i got bad grades or dropped out of stem? i determined a social life was not a risk worth taking, since there was no backup plan available to me.

so i had the most boring college experience possible and now 5 years after college, i make very decent money in a technical field. but instead of playing golf or pickleball or ultimate frisbee in boater shoes like my country club attending WASP-y peers do, i just emulate games from the 2000s (currently playing monster hunter dos) in my pajamas and spend all my spare income trading risky leveraged positions for extra money. because i am basically disillusioned and have no interest in spending any money in hobbies, and i just want to retire as soon as possible. because my life since college has been nothing but grinding, when i work i grind, when i daytrade i grind. so i want to retire as quickly as possible so i get to relax.

my dad has recently started to call me, almost frantic, aggressively asking me why i'm not pursuing a relationship, a wife, a family, and so on. and i really don't know how to explain he basically did everything in his power to create the situation he is not happy with.

62442314
>>62433626
I'm still paying m onthly child support for a 24 year old becaus ethey are in full time education. Just found out this week that they are now doing a masters. I had been looking forward to being emancipated from this, not so much the giving money to my child but having to send it to someone who cheated on me 20 years ago and make the divorce a 10 year long waste of life.

What do I think of this generation that feels they can live off their parents forever? Well I started workinng at 16 and living on my own at 17 (very menial work, cleaning nightclub bathrooms) but as a child I worked beside my father herding farm animals and feeding them every day. My child has never lifted a finger to help me with anything and has in their entire life only worked three months half shift in a shop and walked out of that job because they were asked to mop a floor.

So what are the consequences? Well I am leaving my child nothing in my will but to my fathers relatives. They won't be expecting that but I have never seen that child show the slightest respect for my families heritage or me.

What do I think of zoomers/milennials in general? People like OP? Guess. I don't give a fuck about them and no one should.

62442325
>>62441344
The real losers we met along the way was (you). Pathetic lazy gambling addicts who sat scratching their arses doing pointless degrees and farting on about 'making it'

62442338
>>62442194
You're asw much of a failure as a son as you are as a human being. Fairly typical for a generation to spend a decade on the internet denigrating their parents who wiped their arses and fed them and read them bedtime stories, birthday presents, christmas presents, books, toys, dental etc etc. .

I hope your father disinheirits you you self pitying bum, it is all you deserve. Why should he be loyal to you when you have zero loyalty to him? You're a sickening waste of oxygen.
>>62442194
>(currently playing monster hunter dos) in my pajamas and spend all my spare income trading risky leveraged positions for extra money

62442346
>>62435117
kys do something nice for your parents for once you fucking parasite, I can only assume they tolerate you because they know you are diagnosed mentally disabled

62442364
>>62441222
At a point where every dollar charged means they'll have to pay 2 back to some kike bank.
You can teach that without ruining their start. I started when my daughter was 6 and soon my son will follow: gamified everything, wooden money, savings, deposits, inflation, volume discounts, etc. In a few years we'll be using real markets. And we'll outkike the kikes if needs be, but they're starting their adulthood debt-free provided they grow up responsible. If they waste it then I may change my mind, I guess I'll have to supervise their finance a bit until they convince me they know how to play the game and understand money.

62442366
>>62442346
You first faggot, I’m sure he isn’t a pain to be around and actually might be a joy to his parents which is why they let him live there

62442380
>>62435510
Also all his kids and even his kids spouses work FOR HIM.

Rachel Cruze (daughter) - Co host on his show, has own show her husband Winston Cruze is the CEO of Ramsey Solutions, a financial education and counseling company

Daniel Ramsey - President of his father's company, Ramsey Solutions

Denise Whitmore (daughter) - Director of the Ramsey Family Foundation at Ramsey Solutions

And all three of them OWN Ramsey solutions which is where Dave sells his shit to his viewers

62442389
>>62442338
im not ungrateful to my father at all. you seem to be totally misunderstanding. i am perfectly content with the outcome. i simply reacted to the events in front of me in the ways that made sense with the information i had at the time.

what my post pointa out, i am unsure of how to articulate the idea to my father, who is not happy. "if you wanted a socialite son with WASP-y hobbies and a fiance cut from the same cloth, you probably would have wanted to financially support your son when it mattered - because all the pickleball playing frat bros names Brayden at the country club with picturesque wedding photos , literally all of them, graduated with a 2.1 GPA in business and got their jobs through nepotism". there are no frat bros in boater shoes playing ultimate frisbee whos dads refuse to support them. it doesnt exist.

now, i am not entitled to financial support. just like my dad isnt entitled to grandkids. but these two things are related, even if you want to throw a tantrum and be vulgar. it changes nothing

you seem to be confused and upset. try to detach a bit from your heightened emotional state

62442391
>>62442389
Look at his other post it’s obvious he’s mentally ill and just lashing out at random people on fucking 4 Chan of all places

62442419
>>62442346
Let's take it easy and look first into the mirror before judging others. Not everyone is fit to be a parent. Hopefully your child inherits a good stepfather, which would be a better outcome than inheriting your will.

62442422
>>62442391
what i am trying to articulate in my post might require an IQ of 110 to maybe process. because its slightly nuanced. but its really not that complicated, fundamentally.

my dad wanted a WASP-y son who had fratbro hobbies and a kind of upper class lifestyle. golf, wedding photos, friend group of likeminded frat bros who go to sports games and go out drinking together. but my dad was convinced by talking heads like buffet and ramsey that any financial support for your kids after their early 20s is wrong. so he took that advice and cut off all support. and i, having no support in college, basically had to learn totally by myself how to function and rely on just myself - to the point of becoming self reliant, at the cost of having no appetite for frivolous spending, luxury, or expensive/high maintenance relationships once i entered the workforce. understand- i am not complaining whatsoever, i am just saying, if you want a preppy rich kid socialite son with a hot high maintenance girlfriend, youre probably gonna need to get him a job. if you dont want to because that will spoil him or whatever, thats perfectly fine, just dont be surprised when he finds his own financial advice (eg sam hyde lol), like you found yours, and he decides to go full hermitmaxx mode and he daytrades with his paychecks instead of buying golf clubs or a saving up for an annual trip to europe for his high maintenance hot gf

does this make any sense? am i crazy here?

62442430
>>62442422

son you need to go MAKE SOMETHING of yourself

62442437
>>62442422
Your father is wrong to dictate the life he wants you to live. You are meant to bond with your parents if you both have a good relationship, sometimes helping each other if possible and within reason. Never burden a child like that.

62442543
>>62438432
don't you see the "it is in a different currency"?
it's obviously not in euros u dumbass. European countries have a few more currencies.

62442989
>>62442194
>>62442389
>>62442422
cringe and victimcomplex

62443265
>>62442989
Dads who want to control the lives of their kids without supporting them are just delusional retards as evidenced by the lashing out in response to those posts.

If my boss wants me to do work for him, he pays me. If a business wants me to try their product, they give me incentives for a trial period. If daddy wants his son to live a certain way it's going to take more than a regal command. You wanted to teach your kid self reliance by kicking him out, right? Well now your kid doesn't rely on you and has zero obligation to follow your desired path. Mission accomplished, you successfully removed yourself from having influence.

62443320
>>62442989
>changing his ID
>still trying to lash out at random posters

Yeah the only sad lonely faggot here is you

62443525
26% of US men 25-29 live at home. If you are single and have a good relationship with your family there really is no reason not to do it, you can get some decent money together

62443887
>>62442422
Why not just pay the high quality lifestyle with your own money?

62444170
>>62442422
Buffett gave his kids $90k (roughly $600-700k in today's money) in Berkshire stock when they turned 19.
Peter started a record business, Howard started an excavator business, and I can't remember what Susie did (something involving art galleries), but they all squandered it, with barely a second thought, which is shocking given how much Buffett harps on
>buy and hold
Clearly, he didn't instill that virtue in his own children.
He helped them somewhat in others ways: boguht a farm for Howard, but charged him rent.
He also notoriously would bribe them to not be fat, subjecting them to weighings, as well as offering them rewards if he failed to maintain a given weight. (Not sure if this is based or psychotic.)

62444184
>>62444170
>He helped them somewhat in others ways: boguht a farm for Howard, but charged him rent.
>He also notoriously would bribe them to not be fat, subjecting them to weighings, as well as offering them rewards if he failed to maintain a given weight. (Not sure if this is based or psychotic.)
in the case of the farm, btw, the rent was based in part on how much Howard weighed.
My point was that Buffett actually did give a great deal of assistance to his kids, even if he was a skinflint in other respects.
And his kids mostly squandered it.

62444191
>>62444170
>Buffett gave his kids $90k (roughly $600-700k in today's money) in Berkshire stock when they turned 19.
This was in addition to paying for their college (which none of them finished), btw.

62444231
>>62443887
why would i pay for golf clubs and polos and fancy watches and a WASP-y country club lifestyle when thats not what i personally want?

after a decade of being on my own, i just want to retire as soon as possible and spend as little as possible. even if it means being a single hermit, i dont really care. i was on my own, so i had to determine what was best for me. now, at the eleventh hour, i am being accosted for "being weird" because what i want and what work towards isnt what my dad expected i would do, basically. again, im not complaining. im just raising the questionn, how does one delicately explain that when you give zero suport, you take yourself away from influencing outcomes.

62444276
>>62442989
to be totally honest i see my father as the one was wronged, by the bad parenting advice that comes from financial talking heads like ramsey and buffet who insist to boomers they need to cut off support to their kids at 18. my dad could have had a son with the lifestyle he wanted to see his son have. he just thought it would magically happen with no financial or job finding help. i actually feel very sorrowful with respect to the situation, i think it was a perfectly avoidable, unoptimal outcome for everyone involved.
i dont see myself as a victim at all, i have a very fortunate life in many ways.

>>62442253
The “Gish Gallop” and its prepositional permutations—Gish for Gallop, Gish of Gallop, Gish on Gallop, etc.—mark distinct ontological orientations of rhetorical velocity. To retort technically, one must treat the Gallop not as of Gish but as through Gish: a phenomenon where assertional acceleration surpasses epistemic friction. The Gallop is discourse as kinematic excess—an epistemological overclocking.
Gish for Gallop implies teleology: rhetoric mobilized toward perpetual propulsion. Gish of Gallop encodes possession—the Gallop as Gish’s offspring. Gish on Gallop situates argument atop momentum, while Gish in Gallop dissolves distinction, embedding Gish within motion itself. Gish at Gallop denotes encounter, the cognition of discursive overwhelm. Gish from Gallop reverses causality, rendering the effect its own origin.
Gish about Gallop becomes reflexive critique—commentary risking absorption by its object. Gish under and below Gallop expose the substructure: unspoken premises enabling the deluge. Gish above Gallop implies transcendence, the meta-discursive gaze seeking order in chaos. Gish near and around Gallop orbit the phenomenon, maintaining analytical proximity without dissolution.
Most fertile is Gish between Gallop—a liminal field where assertion and absence interlace, revealing discourse as interval rather than totality. Finally, Gish Gallop stands bare, tautological and absolute: persuasion through proliferation, logos devolved into kinetics. Each form rearticulates the same epistemic entropy—truth displaced by momentum, meaning fragmented by excess. The Gish Gallop, thus, is not fallacy but cosmology: rhetoric as perpetual acceleration toward the vanishing point of comprehension.

62444168
>>62443858
I don't think this is that true anymore, modern AI overlaps self adversarial verification loops to the point where errors are more or less eliminated.

ie. Get 1 AI to code a program, sure there might be bugs, but get another AI to run over night generating test cases, in a loop and testing, supplying feedback to the first AI to make edits etc.

For the price of a human developer for 8 hours of work, you can definitely get more value for money running this setup for 8 hours.


The idea of "I asked chatgpt and it told me there are the wrong number of R's in strawberry" is like 2023 thinking.

Now adays, given enough time the Ai will write a python script that will remove all r's in a word, count length and determine how many Rs went missing. It's capable of cyclically problem solving reasoning and building and running tools to verify its own answers autonomously.


It still makes mistakes but so do people.

>>62443768
Well, you get the AGI to look at your factory and optimise the shit out of it so your way more efficient then your competition allowing you to underpriced them and kill them.


Ie. An AI optimised car factory designs a new layout the same way it can win a chess game or Go game, this new layout allows 15% more cars made with the same resources. So we can sell the car for 15% cheaper.

Suddenly more customers, and the other competing company goes out of business.


This but everyone doing it at the same time in some kind of arms race.
